Based on the provided data, the following tests cover the requirement "The HES in the production environment shall be designed for at least 50 simultaneous Users without affecting the performance of the Metering System."

- T-4: Log date and system time (T-1)
- T-4: Send the same info again (T-2)
- T-4: Traceable metering devices (T-3)
- T-4: 50 simultaneous users (T-5)
- T-5: Run operations with a logged-in user (T-4)
- T-5: Run the same operations with 50 concurrent users (T-5)
- T-15: Record communication errors (T-15)
- T-16: Configure records to be stored for events (T-16)
- T-17: Request further confirmation (T-13)
- T-18: Response times in the user interface (T-18)
- T-19: PQ values to HES within 4h (T-19)
- T-20: Type approval - Cat1 (T-20)
- T-21: Type approval - Cat2 (T-21)
- T-22: Location of the marking - Cat1 (T-22)
- T-23: Location of the label - Cat2 (T-23)
- T-24: Screw anchors - Cat1 (T-24)
- T-25: Screw anchors - Cat2 (T-25)
- T-26: Quantity of allowed faulty meters Cat1 - RISE (T-26)
- T-27: Quantity of allowed faulty meters Cat2 - RISE (T-27)
- T-28: Lifetime Cat2 (T-28)
- T-29: Design of the solution (T-29)
- T-30: Logging of events (T-30)

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification.

The following tests cover the requirement "In HES, it should be possible to see whether the Customer Interface in a Metering Unit is enabled or disabled for data sending."

- T-17: Verify that the Customer Interface in a Metering Unit is enabled or disabled for data sending by checking the status in HES.
- T-18: Request further confirmation before executing commands that may have serious consequences.
- T-15: Verify that all metrics (PQ) are time-stamped with the Current time when received by HES.
- T-16: Verify that commands, initiated by the User in HES, which may have serious consequences in case of incorrect handling (deletion of stored measurement and structure data and switching off and on of power) require additional confirmation before being executed.
- T-1: Verify that the Customer Interface in a Metering Unit is enabled or disabled for data sending by checking the status in HES.

These tests cover the requirement.

The following tests cover the requirement "Log date and system time":

- T-0: Security log
- T-1: Log date and system time
- T-15: Record communication errors
- T-16: Configure records to be stored for events
- T-17: Status of the customer interface
- T-18: Response times in the user interface
- T-19: PQ values to HES within 4h
- T-20: Type approval - Cat1
- T-21: Type approval - Cat2
- T-22: Location of the marking - Cat1
- T-23: Location of the label - Cat2
- T-24: Screw anchors - Cat1
- T-25: Screw anchors - Cat2
- T-26: Quantity of allowed faulty meters Cat1 - RISE
- T-27: Quantity of allowed faulty meters Cat2 - RISE
- T-28: Lifetime Cat2
- T-30: Design of the solution

These tests cover the requirement by verifying that the system logs the date and system time for each event and that the events and their attribute values can be displayed in an event list. Additionally, tests T-15, T-16, and T-17 verify that the system can record and store information related to communication failures, configuration settings, and status of the customer interface, respectively. Test T-20 and T-21 ensure that the Extended Type Approval and Certification of Metering Units is carried out according to method SPM 1618, which includes testing with 12 kV surge voltage. Test T-22 and T-23 verify that there are no elevations around the Metering Unit's screw attachments that limit the size of the screw head. Test T-26 and T-27 ensure that the occurrence of faults does not exceed 0.75% per year of the installed volume. Test T-28 verifies that a Metering Unit has a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE. Test T-30 verifies the design of the solution in comparison to the requirements in 2.1 Technical solution.
